title: phlegmariurus nilagiricus
intro: Phlegmariurus nilagiricus, also known as Nilagiri climbing fern, is a type of vascular plant that belongs to the lycopodiopsida class and is native to the Western Ghats region of India. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The plant is believed to provide various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and promoting wound healing. Traditionally, the plant is prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions for its medicinal uses, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential side effects.
